description_long: This object is a botanical specimen featuring a cluster of white, unopened flower buds attached to a green stem. The buds are elongated and oval, with smooth surfaces and subtle greenish tips, suggesting they are not yet in full bloom. The stem is slender and segmented, branching out to support multiple buds at varying heights and angles. A few small, lanceolate leaves are visible, closely hugging the stem and partially obscured by the buds. The overall structure is upright and compact, with the buds arranged in a somewhat spiral pattern around the central stem. The coloration is predominantly white with hints of pale green, giving the object a fresh and natural appearance.
description: A cluster of white, unopened flower buds on a green stem with a few small leaves. The buds are oval and smooth, arranged spirally around the stem.
description_view_0: Front view showing the densest cluster of white buds and the main stem with several buds facing forward.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the staggered arrangement of buds and a clearer view of the small leaves attached to the stem.
description_view_2: Angled view displaying the overlapping buds and the curvature of the stem, with some buds partially hidden.
description_view_3: Rear view revealing the back row of buds and the branching structure of the stem, with leaves more visible.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a spiral configuration around the central stem, with each bud showing overlapping, tightly closed petals that are not yet separated or fully visible. The arrangement suggests a whorled or spiral phyllotaxy typical of many inflorescences.
